title: S3a: widen the ba-nine-section classifier — the 'shapeless area' verdict was itself an artifact of blindness
action: GATE-DEFINITION CELL, inserted ahead of f2-3 which returned [BLOCKED] with the evidence. The shipped classifier requires bare anchor ids (/^\*\*(B\d+)\s+—/ and /^-\s+(R\d+)\s+—/), but five remaining areas write rules as '- **R1** — ...' with the id bold-wrapped: onboarding 28, doctrine-layer 17, feedback-digest 15, performance-log 11, decision-memory 9 — all currently derive R0. doctrine-layer inventories 20 anchors with 21 UNPARSED blocks (more unparsed than parsed). Critically, decision-memory was classified 'shapeless, 0 anchors' by the F9 planning sweep and slated for a special scheme in S5 — it is NOT shapeless; its 9 rules were invisible. The shapeless verdict was an artifact of the blindness, so this cell also RE-CLASSIFIES the migration order. Do: (1) widen the ba-nine-section classifier to accept bold-wrapped ids and letter-suffixed ids — /^\*\*(B\d+[a-z]?)\s+—/ and /^-\s+\*{0,2}(R\d+[a-z]?)\*{0,2}\s+—/ — and any equivalent form the sweep in (3) reveals. Keep the unparsed accounting exact. (2) PROVE THE NO-OP ON SHIPPED PINS: advisor-protocol must still derive exactly 26 {4,9,6,7} with unparsed_blocks 0 and critical-patterns exactly 47 — from their pinned blobs, with expected_counts unchanged. If either moves, the widening is wrong; narrow it until both are strict no-ops. This is the single most important assertion in the cell. (3) RE-INVENTORY ALL 9 remaining areas after widening and record, per area, counts + unparsed_blocks + unparsed_lines, in a report at docs/history/okf-migration-f2/reports/inventory-sweep.md. Then classify each area honestly: CONFORMING (unparsed_blocks small relative to anchors) or GENUINELY SHAPELESS (no anchor-bearing sections at all — the advisor named worktree-parallelism as the likely real case). (4) The fidelity floor must still pass on both shipped areas unedited (min/median quoted). (5) Add a regression test asserting the bold-id and letter-suffix forms are classified, so a future narrowing is caught. RED-FIRST: the widening tests fail before the change; capture verbatim.

truths: red evidence: the widening tests failed before the change (verbatim)
STRICT NO-OP PROVEN: advisor-protocol still 26 {4,9,6,7} unparsed_blocks 0, critical-patterns still 47 — from the pinned blobs, expected_counts untouched — quoted in the trace
doctrine-layer's post-widening inventory quoted (expected ~39 anchors, ~2 unparsed) alongside its pre-widening 20/21
the 9-area sweep report exists at docs/history/okf-migration-f2/reports/inventory-sweep.md with counts + unparsed + an honest CONFORMING/SHAPELESS verdict per area
fidelity floor still passes on both shipped areas with zero concept edits — min/median quoted
a regression test asserts bold-wrapped and letter-suffixed ids are classified
prohibitions: never widen so far that a shipped pin's derived counts move — the no-op is the safety property
do not author any area concepts in this cell — gate definition only
do not edit any docs/knowledge/ concept
do not lower the fidelity floor or edit expected_counts to accommodate the widening

trace.outcome: widened the ba-nine-section classifier to the id forms the specs actually use; strict no-op on both shipped pins (26 and 47, expected_counts untouched); 9-area sweep re-classified decision-memory as CONFORMING and worktree-parallelism as the only genuinely shapeless area
